fields.description:
# op-cert: trusted gate service ‚ÄĒ authoritative-state fetch, per-kind dispatch, async job API (+ client command)

## Why
For high assurance, a developer routes a gate run to a trusted environment that fetches
authoritative state ITSELF (ticket state from the tickets branch; code at the review remote's
`main` tip), runs the gate, and returns an Ed25519-signed `rebar.opcert.v1` cert ‚ÄĒ so
client-supplied inputs cannot forge a pass (the SLSA non-falsifiable-provenance principle). This
story builds the **service and its client half only**; AWS IaC + the real deploy are the sibling
"AWS IaC + deploy" story, and the signing behavior itself ships in the "producer signing seam"
story this depends on. All design forks are settled in the epic's "Producer-signing &
compute-runtime design resolution" section ‚ÄĒ this story implements, it does not decide.

**Hard dependencies (not yet on `main`):** the op-cert substrate consumed here
(`signing.sign_opcert_manifest`, `src/rebar/attest/opcert.py`, `trusted_env.py`, `sshsig.sign` ‚ÄĒ
Gerrit changes 649/650/660, in review), the merge-gate consumer `rebar verify-opcert` (story 4214,
Gerrit change 664, in review ‚ÄĒ referenced by the client ACs below), and the producer signing seam
story must land first;
mentions of them below are forward references to that stack, enforced by this ticket's
`depends_on` links. `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py` (the pattern mirrored) IS on `main` today.

## What
**Service** (`src/rebar/opcert_service/`, library-mode: rebar imported behind a thin FastAPI app ‚ÄĒ
the `review_bot/app.py` software pattern; NOT the stdio `rebar-mcp`):
- **Async job API** (gate runs take 30s‚Äďminutes; no sync request can hold): `POST /opcert/jobs`
  validates, enqueues on an in-process `asyncio.Queue`, returns **202 `{job_id}`**;
  `GET /opcert/jobs/{job_id}` returns the job record. Single background worker drains serially
  under a bounded per-run timeout: default **900 s** (gate runs take 30s‚Äďminutes; the ceiling is
  generous), configurable via `REBAR_OPCERT_JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`. Job state is in-memory (a restart loses queued jobs; clients
  re-submit ‚ÄĒ cert minting is idempotent).
- **Request schema**: `{"ticket_id": str, "kind": "completion-verifier"|"plan-review"}` ‚ÄĒ ONLY
  identifiers. Any other client field (a `material_fingerprint`, a `commit`, an `env_id`) is
  IGNORED; the server derives every authoritative value itself.
- **Authoritative fetch**: the worker fetches the tickets branch (ticket state) and the review
  remote's `main` tip (code) with its own read-only credentials ‚ÄĒ sources named by env config:
  `REBAR_OPCERT_REVIEW_REMOTE_URL` (code) and `REBAR_OPCERT_TICKETS_REMOTE_URL` (tickets
  branch), credentials from the service env. It sets `merged_log_commit = <fetched main HEAD
  sha>` and computes the material fingerprint itself. Each job runs in an **ephemeral
  workspace clone** discarded after the job.
- **Per-kind dispatch + native verdicts (no unified enum)**: `plan-review` ‚Üí
  `rebar.llm.review_plan(...)` (verdict `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE`); `completion-verifier` ‚Üí
  `rebar.llm.verify_completion(...)` (verdict `PASS|FAIL`). A raised
  `LLMError`/`LLMUnavailableError` maps to the job's transport status ‚ÄĒ never a fabricated
  verdict. Response/job schema:
  `{status: "queued"|"running"|"completed"|"error", kind, verdict: <native enum>|null,
  envelope: <encoded DSSE>|null, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit, error: {class, message}|null}`
  ‚ÄĒ `envelope` present ONLY on a `PASS` verdict. `error.class` is a CLOSED enum mapped from the
  raised exception type: `"llm_unavailable"` (`LLMUnavailableError`), `"llm_error"` (other
  `LLMError`), `"timeout"` (per-run timeout exceeded), `"internal"` (anything else).
- **Signing = the producer seam, once; the service only PROVISIONS the key.** The gate
  producers mint the op-cert via the signing seam (producer-seam story); the handler itself
  never calls a sign function and never passes `sign=False`. The hand-off protocol: this story
  adds one override to the seam's key resolution ‚ÄĒ `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` (an explicit key
  file path) takes precedence over the `<tracker>/.opcert-key` genesis. Before dispatching a
  job, the worker fetches the SSM SecureString (`/rebar/prod/opcert-ed25519-key`),
  **materializes it to a 0600 `mkstemp` temp file** (SSHSIG signing shells
  `ssh-keygen -Y sign -f <path>` and needs a file path), sets `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` to that
  path and `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` as the principal for the gate run (the principal is the
  environment's logical id string ‚ÄĒ it MUST exactly equal the environment's `env_id` entry in
  the consuming project's `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`, since it becomes the DSSE keyid
  the merge gate pins on; a non-empty printable token, e.g. `nava-opcert-prod-1`), and **deletes the file in a
  `finally`** ‚ÄĒ never persisted, and the seam signs exactly once with the provisioned key. The
  key MUST be passphrase-free (`ssh-keygen -Y sign` is non-interactive). The legacy HMAC
  environment key is never provisioned in the server.
- **Stateless / store-read-only ‚ÄĒ reconciled with the seam's `sign=True`.** Running a gate with
  signing on appends a `SIGNATURE` event to the store it runs against; on the server that store
  is the job's ephemeral workspace clone, with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and NO tickets-branch
  write credential ‚ÄĒ so the event lands only in the discarded workspace, never on the shared
  branch. The worker extracts the envelope from the workspace's freshly-appended record and
  returns it in the response; the CLIENT persists it to the shared tickets branch. Nothing is
  ever pushed by the server (asserted by a test on the workspace remote config). Endpoint authn
  (SigV4) is terminated at API Gateway (the IaC story); the app additionally requires a shared
  secret as defense in depth ‚ÄĒ same posture as `review_bot`'s check (which uses a `?token=`
  query parameter, `review_bot/app.py`); the opcert app checks an `X-Opcert-Guard` **header**
  instead (value from the `REBAR_OPCERT_GUARD` env var, materialized from SSM by the deploy
  story), because API Gateway injects static request headers on the integration.
- **Container image**: `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` (build context = repo root, per the
  review_bot Dockerfile pattern) installing **OpenSSH ‚Č• 8.9** and running
  `uvicorn rebar.opcert_service.app:app`.

**Client** (`src/rebar/_commands/remote_cert.py`, CLI `rebar remote-cert <ticket-id> <kind>`):
- Reads `verify.opcert_remote_url` (new `VerifyConfig` key, `str | None = None`; unset ‚Üí clear
  error; nothing remote is ever required for local operation). SigV4-signs the request with
  ambient AWS credentials, submits the job, polls to a terminal status, and on `PASS` **persists
  the returned envelope + bound fields as the `SIGNATURE` event** (the same event shape
  `sign_opcert_manifest` appends; auto-pushed with the tickets branch). Safe because the envelope
  is self-authenticating ‚ÄĒ tampering with the bound values breaks the signature the merge gate
  (`rebar verify-opcert`) verifies. Non-PASS / error ‚Üí non-zero exit with the job's error class.

## Scope
- In: the FastAPI service, dispatch/verdict mapping, authoritative fetch, SSM-parameter key
  materialization + the `REBAR_OPCERT_KEY_PATH` seam override, Dockerfile, the `remote-cert` client command, `verify.opcert_remote_url`.
- Out: Terraform/API Gateway/SSM resources + the real deploy + live round-trip
  (the "AWS IaC + deploy" story); the producer signing seam itself (dependency); HMAC removal
  (8f1d).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /opcert/jobs` ACKs 202 `{job_id}` and `GET /opcert/jobs/{id}` reaches a terminal
      status via the background worker; a test drives the round-trip against a fake authoritative
      source with the request/response schemas above.
- [ ] Integrity test (the load-bearing one): a request carrying a **doctored
      `material_fingerprint`** and a doctored `commit` is accepted, the extra fields are ignored,
      and the signed op-cert's bound `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit` equal the values
      the server derived from its own fetch ‚ÄĒ a client cannot influence any bound value.
- [ ] Per-kind verdict mapping: tests assert `plan-review` jobs surface
      `PASS|BLOCK|INDETERMINATE` natively, `completion-verifier` jobs surface `PASS|FAIL`, a
      raised `LLMError` yields `status="error"` with `verdict=null`, and `envelope` is non-null
      ONLY on `PASS` (BLOCK/FAIL/INDETERMINATE/error all return `envelope: null`).
- [ ] Key materialization: a test asserts the SSM SecureString value is written to a 0600
      `mkstemp` file for the `ssh-keygen -Y sign` invocation and that NO key file remains after
      signing (including on a raised exception ‚ÄĒ the `finally` path).
- [ ] The server appends no tickets-branch event: a test asserts the store is unchanged after a
      completed job; `rebar remote-cert` persists the returned envelope as a `SIGNATURE` event
      that `verify_signature`/`rebar verify-opcert` then certify, and a tampered envelope is
      rejected (`mismatch`).
- [ ] Offline default preserved: a test asserts every local op-cert sign/verify path works with
      `verify.opcert_remote_url` unset and no network ‚ÄĒ the remote path is opt-in and its absence
      never blocks a local operation.
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` installs OpenSSH ‚Č• 8.9 and starts the service; a test
      asserts the Dockerfile declares both.
- [ ] Origin guard: tests assert a request missing the `X-Opcert-Guard` header, or carrying a
      value that does not match `REBAR_OPCERT_GUARD`, is rejected (403) before any work is
      enqueued; a matching header is accepted.
- [ ] Server never pushes: a test asserts the job workspace runs with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and
      no push remote/credential, and that the shared store is byte-identical after a completed
      PASS job (the SIGNATURE event exists only in the discarded workspace).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
